zh.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield於大約1599年出生在英國。他於從1620年到1622年與Dorothy Cotton在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1636年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德被列為first settled minister of the town of wethersfield。他去世於1648年8月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年50歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德維利亞 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield于大约1599年出生在英国。他于从1620年到1622年与Dorothy Cotton在英国结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他于1636年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德被列为first settled minister of the town of wethersfield。他去世于1648年8月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年50岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德維利亞 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield was born about 1599, in England. He married Dorothy Cotton from 1620 to 1622, in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters. In 1636, at the age of 40, his occupation is listed as first settled minister of the town of wethersfield in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. He died on 9 August 1648, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 50, and was buried in Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, United States.

En 1636, à l’âge de 40 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est first settled minister of the town of wethersfield à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé le 9 août 1648, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield è nato il circa 1599 nel England. Lui sposato Dorothy Cotton da 1620 a 1622, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie. Nel 1636, all'età di 40 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come first settled minister of the town of wethersfield nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Morì il 9 agosto 1648, nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfieldは約1599年にイギリスで生まれた。から1620年まで1622年にイギリスでDorothy Cottonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。1636年，40歳の時，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドでの職業はfirst settled minister of the town of wethersfieldと記録されている。彼は1648年8月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで50歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドヴィラッジ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Bri: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드
ko.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield(은)는 대략 1599년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1620년 부터 1622년 까지 영국에서 Dorothy Cotton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1636년, 40세의 나이로 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 first settled minister of the town of wethersfield이었다. 그는 1648년 8월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드 비라지 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield nasceu aproximadamente 1599, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Dorothy Cotton de 1620 a 1622, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas. Em 1636, com 40 anos, sua profissão aparece como first settled minister of the town of wethersfield em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em 9 de agosto de 1648, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield родился около 1599 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothy Cotton с 1620 по 1622 в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей. В 1636 году, в возрасте 40 лет,  он работал по профессии first settled minister of the town of wethersfield в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер 9 августа 1648 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности вилладж кеметири, ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield nació aproximadamente 1599, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Dorothy Cotton desde 1620 hasta 1622, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 6 hijas. En 1636, a la edad de 40 años, su ocupación registrada era first settled minister of the town of wethersfield en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Murió el 9 de agosto de 1648, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
